Patch Set 1:

test 106 in conf-sanity.sh was added along with original re-read patch. I'd expect it to fail. In test it says: "ASSERTION osp_sync_thread() ( thread->t_flags != SVC_RUNNING ) failed shows that osp code is buggy"
Meanwhile conf-sanity.sh with SLOW is not running usually, so we might miss that. Janitor runs it but result are strange, it reports PASSED but from log there are ENOSPC errors so test might be just skipped after all

Patch Set 1:

http://testing.linuxhacker.ru/lustre-reports/24554/testresults/conf-sanity-slow-106-zfs-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/

It seems here we faced that issue: "llog process with osp_sync_process_queues failed: -53" just before crash

Patch Set 1:

> http://testing.linuxhacker.ru/lustre-reports/24554/testresults/conf-sanity-slow-106-zfs-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/
 > 
 > It seems here we faced that issue: "llog process with
 > osp_sync_process_queues failed: -53" just before crash

Ok, I initially saw that on my patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/45708/ (sanity test 60a is failing -> llog_test_10)
This patch modifies the behavior for a wrapped catalog (try to read more record when reading the beginning of the file.

Abandoned

Abandon this patch in favor of the https://review.whamcloud.com/48070/ ("LU-16052 llog: handle -EBADR for catalog processing")

commit.subject: LU-15938 llog: Fix chunk re-read case in llog_process_thread
commit.message:
LU-15938 llog: Fix chunk re-read case in llog_process_thread

The patch 1a24dcd changed the return code (-EIO -> -EBADR) of
llog_osd_next_block() when it reachs the end of a llog file.
This break the re-read logic of the last chunk if new record had been
added during the first read.

Fixes: 1a24dcd ("LU-15938 lod: prevent endless retry in recovery thread")
Signed-off-by: Etienne AUJAMES <eaujames@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Ide142cc7f28c5c520bb78d671b494a166e46e49f
